content.claim: Institutionalized stakeholder-consultation mechanisms in Hungarian school governance were suspended or curtailed after 2010 as part of broader centralization, affecting the procedural architecture available for local-level input in school-network rationalization decisions.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Sources do not specify which particular consultation mechanisms were removed or how they functioned prior to removal
'Curtailment' is asserted but not operationally defined (e.g., reduced frequency vs. complete elimination)
No documentation of informal or alternative consultation channels that may have emerged after 2010
Causal link between broader regime centralization and school-governance consultation is inferred, not directly demonstrated
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
